mnemonics.title: Port Number Ranges
mnemonics.content: 'Famous Servers Need Registration: Famous (0-1023), Servers (1024-49151), Need (49152-65535), Registration' - Remember: Well-known, Registered, Dynamic/Ephemeral ports.

mnemonics.title: TCP vs UDP Services
mnemonics.content: 'TCP is Reliable, Ordered, Connected; UDP is Unreliable, Unordered, Unconnected' - First letters: TR-OC vs UU (sounds like 'you').

mnemonics.title: Transport Layer Responsibilities
mnemonics.content: 'MESSAGE SEND PACKET: Multiplexing, End-to-end, Sequencing, Acknowledgment, Error control, Flow control, Port addressing, Application interface, Congestion control, Tunneling' - Core transport services.

mnemonics.title: Socket Definition
mnemonics.content: 'SOCKET = Server's IP + Server's Port (together identify unique endpoint)' - Think of socket as the 'address' where your application 'listens' or 'connects'.

acronyms.usage: Point where a layer provides services to the layer above; at transport layer, identified by port numbers.
Transport layer SAP - essentially a port number that identifies a specific application process.
Network layer SAP - typically an IP address that identifies a specific host.
Connection-oriented, reliable transport protocol providing byte-stream service.
Connectionless, unreliable transport protocol providing datagram service.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a building (OSI model). Application layer is the top floor (residents), Network layer is the lobby (door man/routing). Transport layer is the elevator operator - directs messages to correct rooms (ports) within the building.

Think of a hotel (host) with many rooms (applications). The concierge (transport layer) collects mail from all rooms (multiplexing) and delivers incoming mail to correct rooms (demultiplexing) using room numbers (ports).
A socket pair is like a phone call: Caller ID + Phone Number (Source Socket) ↔ Receiver ID + Phone Number (Destination Socket). Both needed for unique communication.

analogies.analogy: Like a postal service within a country. The postal service (transport layer) handles sorting (multiplexing), addressing (port numbers), guaranteed delivery (reliable transport), and delivery confirmation (acknowledgments), while the road network (network layer) handles actual routing.
Port numbers are like apartment numbers in a large building. The street address (IP) gets you to the building, but you need the apartment number (port) to reach the specific resident (application).

Connection-oriented is like a phone call - you dial (connect), talk (data transfer), then hang up (disconnect). Connectionless is like sending a letter - you write address, drop in mailbox, no connection maintained.
